generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance PositionEncoderDrive1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder mounted on motor for real-time positional feedback in manufacturing robotic system. Part of two-position encoder setup per drive axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderDataDrive1
]

instance PositionEncoderDrive2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder mounted on gearbox for real-time positional feedback in manufacturing robotic system. Part of two-position encoder setup per drive axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderDataDrive2
]

instance PositionEncoderDrive3 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder mounted on motor for real-time positional feedback in manufacturing robotic system. Part of two-position encoder setup per drive axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderDataDrive3
]

instance PositionEncoderDrive4 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder mounted on gearbox for real-time positional feedback in manufacturing robotic system. Part of two-position encoder setup per drive axis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderDataDrive4
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance PositionEncoderDataDrive1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Time-series positional data streamed via MQTT from motor-mounted position encoder for drive axis 1. Part of automated one-way data flow to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderDrive1
]

instance PositionEncoderDataDrive2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Time-series positional data streamed via MQTT from gearbox-mounted position encoder for drive axis 1. Part of automated one-way data flow to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderDrive2
]

instance PositionEncoderDataDrive3 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Time-series positional data streamed via MQTT from motor-mounted position encoder for drive axis 2. Part of automated one-way data flow to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderDrive3
]

instance PositionEncoderDataDrive4 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Time-series positional data streamed via MQTT from gearbox-mounted position encoder for drive axis 2. Part of automated one-way data flow to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderDrive4
]

instance AggregatedPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data trace combining all position encoder streams for purpose-driven analysis. Implies preprocessing of raw sensor inputs via implicit filtering/normalization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionEncoderDataDrive1, PositionEncoderDataDrive2, PositionEncoderDataDrive3, PositionEncoderDataDrive4
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLFunctionalArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram representing functional architecture of robotic system with state machines (SysML v1.7) for logical behavior modeling."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance SysMLLogicalArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram representing logical architecture components, interfaces, and connectors for robotic system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model implementing detailed behavioral dynamics of robotic system including kinematics and control logic."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance PositionalTimeSeriesData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed time-series positional data aggregated from all encoder streams with implicit preprocessing for downstream analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AggregatedPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance ContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Contextual data trace representing operational states and positional feedback for specific manufacturing analytics purpose."
    DTDFVocab:fromData AggregatedPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message Queuing Telemetry Transport protocol enabling real-time or near-real-time streaming of sensor data from physical system to digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTransmissionService
]

instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling tool enabling creation and management of functional and logical architecture models for robotic system."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink simulation environment enabling detailed behavioral modeling and dynamic analysis of robotic system."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MBSE-based data aggregation framework using architecture description languages (SysML IBDs) to integrate heterogeneous data sources for contextual analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataTransmissionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated one-way data transmission service enabling real-time streaming of sensor data from physical system to digital shadow via MQTT protocol."
    DTDFVocab:provides PositionalDataInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking and aggregation service for contextual data traces from physical robotic system enabling passive observation without feedback."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStateInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data aggregation and abstraction service providing dashboard or contextualized representation of robotic system operational data for stakeholders."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizedStateInsight
]

// C17: Insights
instance PositionalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight derived from processed time-series positional data enabling analysis of robotic system kinematic behavior and operational states."
]

instance OperationalStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight representing aggregated operational states and contextual data traces for manufacturing analytics purposes."
]

instance VisualizedStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of robotic system operational data enabling passive stakeholder observation without system modification."
]

// C1: Environment
instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial manufacturing environment containing robotic system with digital shadow for automated data collection and contextual analysis."
    base:contains RoboticSystemComponent
]

// C2: Components
instance RoboticSystemComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical robotic system component within manufacturing environment with integrated position encoders for digital shadow interaction."
    base:isContainedIn ManufacturingEnvironment
]

// C12: Digital Shadow
instance FrictionShadow : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Passive digital shadow implementation for single robotic axis in manufacturing context following Digital Shadow Reference Model. Enables one-way data flow from physical system to digital representation."
    base:contains PositionalTimeSeriesData, ContextualDataTrace
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment ManufacturingEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocol, DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates within a **real-time data processing framework**, where data traces are collected and processed with fine-grained temporal granularity to ensure traceability. The system leverages **data series** and **data traces** for continuous monitoring and aggregation, enabling real-time abstraction and processing of physical system states. While the documents emphasize the importance of **temporal consistency** in data processing pipelines (e.g., via processing components consuming input data traces and producing output traces), no explicit quantitative metrics (e.g., synchronization frequencies, latency requirements, or time-scale thresholds) are provided. The system relies on **transitive traceability** to maintain temporal alignment between physical and digital representations, ensuring that computed data traces retain their origin and dependencies. The processing logic, encoded in simulation or data calculation models, operates within a **real-time processing paradigm** to support dynamic updates and abstractions of the physical system’s state."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** in the documents is orchestrated through a **reference model-based approach**, where the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** serves as a foundational framework for concretizing domain-specific Digital Twin architectures. The model emphasizes **component integration** via **processing components** (e.g., simulation tools, data calculation models) that operate on data traces with **fine-grained traceability** to their origins. The **conformance relation** ensures that concrete realizations adhere to the reference model’s structure, enabling **system-wide coordination** through standardized associations between classes (e.g., data traces, processing logic). While no explicit **architecture patterns** (e.g., microservices, event-driven) are mentioned, the use of **heterogeneous tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, C++) implies a **modular and extensible** constellation. Resource management is implied through the **purpose-driven design**, where Digital Twins are customized to specific goals, but no **resource allocation mechanisms** (e.g., load balancing, QoS policies) are detailed. The model does not specify **orchestration technologies** (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker) or **inter-component communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, gRPC)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    The document discusses **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** approaches for describing system structure and behavior, which implicitly outlines lifecycle stages for Digital Twin development:

    - **Development Stages**:
      1. **Requirements Phase**: Defines system needs and constraints.
      2. **Functional Architecture**: Breaks down system functions (e.g., using RFLP methodology).
      3. **Logical Architecture**: Describes components, connectors, and interfaces (e.g., via ADLs like AADL, SysML IBDs).
      4. **Product/Technical Layer**: Integrates physical/technical details (e.g., simulation tools like Simulink or code in C++/Java).

    - **Representation Types**:
      - **Modeling Languages**: SysML, ADLs (e.g., ACME, MontiArc), and state machines for behavior.
      - **Simulation Tools**: MathWorks Simulink, other simulation methods, and general-purpose programming languages.
      - **Heterogeneous Data**: Aggregated from multiple tools/notations (e.g., requirements, functional, logical, physical).

    - **Lifecycle Governance**:
      - **Integration of Stakeholders**: Addresses diverse perspectives (e.g., engineering, manufacturing) via MBSE frameworks.
      - **Abstraction Layers**: Manages complexity through layered modeling (e.g., RFLP’s Requirements → Functions → Logics → Physics).
      - **Transition Management**: Implicit in iterative refinement (e.g., moving from logical to technical layers).

    **Note**: The document does not explicitly define a *Digital Twin-specific* lifecycle (e.g., creation, operation, retirement) but aligns with broader **MBSE lifecycle** principles. The **Digital Shadow** is introduced as a one-way data flow (passive), contrasting with active DTs (which may include lifecycle management)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system prioritizes **model fidelity** through **multi-layered abstraction hierarchies** in MBSE, ensuring consistency from requirements to physical implementations. Validation and verification are implicitly addressed via **architecture description languages (ADLs)** and **behavioral modeling tools** (e.g., SysML state machines, Simulink) to detect inconsistencies across layers. While the document does not explicitly outline **quantitative uncertainty sources or error correction mechanisms**, it references **friction models** as a means to improve accuracy in physical simulations. Quality assurance is inherently supported by the **structured MBSE workflow**, which mandates cross-tool validation through **integrated modeling notations** (e.g., SysML, MontiArc)."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin system is explicitly limited to **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** as the communication protocol for streaming sensor data from physical objects to the digital shadow. No other protocols (e.g., OPC UA, CoAP, HTTP) or network topologies (e.g., mesh, star) are mentioned. The document does not provide details on bandwidth requirements, latency constraints, or redundancy mechanisms. Security protocols are also not addressed. The system’s communication is described as a **unidirectional flow** with no bidirectional control or feedback loops, aligning with the digital shadow’s passive nature. Visualization of the data is referenced via a **dashboard**, but no technical specifics about the underlying network architecture or connectivity are provided."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The deployment infrastructure for this Digital Twin system is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. However, the system leverages **MathWorks Simulink** for executing **executable simulation models** (e.g., friction modeling) to represent physical behaviors in a digital format. The document mentions **dashboard-based visualization** but does not specify hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise), computational/storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, or deployment models (e.g., containerization, orchestration). No references to cloud providers (AWS, Azure), edge devices, or orchestration tools (Docker, Kubernetes) are present. The focus remains on **model-based systems engineering (MBSE)** and **digital shadow** concepts rather than deployment infrastructure."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with external systems through **Asset Administration Shells (AAS)**, a standardized interoperability framework for Industry 4.0, and employs **MQTT** as a data exchange protocol for transferring data traces between components. While the documents do not explicitly enumerate all external dependencies or integration patterns, the use of AAS and MQTT aligns with **enterprise system interoperability standards** and **real-time data transfer mechanisms**. The system’s design supports **purpose-driven integration**, where data traces are aggregated and processed for specific applications, but no further details on broader horizontal integration (e.g., with other Digital Twins or legacy systems) are provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
    The document references **industry standards and frameworks** relevant to Digital Twin/Digital Shadow development:

    - **Modeling Standards**:
      - **SysML [17]**: Systems Modeling Language for MBSE (functional/logical architecture).
      - **ADLs**: ACME [23], AADL [24], MontiArc [25]-[26] for component/connector modeling.
      - **State Machines**: SysML v1.7 for behavioral description.

    - **Simulation Tools**:
      - **MathWorks Simulink [28]**: Industry-standard for dynamic system simulation.
      - **General-Purpose Languages**: C++, Java for behavioral implementation.

    - **Interoperability**:
      - **Heterogeneous Integration**: MBSE frameworks (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) address **cross-tool compatibility** (e.g., requirements ↔ simulation models).
      - **Not Explicit**: No mention of **Digital Twin-specific** interoperability standards (e.g., OPC UA, ISO/IEC 23026).

    - **Certification/Compliance**:
      - **Not Addressed**: No references to certification bodies (e.g., ISO 23026 for DTs) or compliance frameworks.

    - **Digital Shadow Context**:
      - **Kritzinger et al. [32]**: Defines shadows as **purpose-specific data aggregation**, implying alignment with **manufacturing data standards** (e.g., OPC UA for Industry 4.0).
      - **Active DTs**: May require **additional standards** (e.g., for real-time analytics or feedback loops), but not detailed here."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for robotic friction modeling (as referenced in [45]) incorporates safety-critical constraints through the physical robot's safety cell, which restricts the maximum working range of the robotic arm to the angular limits of [-90°, 90°]. This ensures operational safety by preventing hazardous movements outside predefined boundaries.

In the context of the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) framework ([46], [47], [50]), security considerations are implied through standardized data modeling, where digital shadows (as described in [42]) require secure communication channels for real-time data synchronization across the automation pyramid. While explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication) is absent, the use of MQTT ([52]) for data streaming in model-driven engineering ([43]) suggests a need for secure communication channels to prevent unauthorized access to sensor data or control signals. The AAS structure itself, with its semantic asset modeling, implies potential for role-based access control (RBAC) mechanisms to govern data access permissions within the Digital Twin ecosystem. However, no specific implementation details (e.g., OAuth, TLS, or firewall rules) are provided in the documents."
]
